notes: Aramid fiber (Kevlar 29/49) spall liner. ~0.15-0.2x RHA per mm vs KE (spall liner - not designed for direct impact). Primarily catches fragments from penetrated armor. Kevlar 29 tensile 3.6 GPa, density 1.44 g/cm³. Proven in M113A3 LFTE (ADA267255). Modern IFVs use UHMWPE or hybrid liners for superior weight efficiency. US DoD studies show ~35% reduction in spall injuries with aramid liners.

source.reference: DuPont Kevlar technical datasheet, US DoD ADA267255
source.methodology: RHAe from ARL ballistic test data on Kevlar spall liners vs fragments. Tensile and density per DuPont Kevlar 29 specification. Spall reduction per DoD study data.
